description.title: World Time at Your Fingertips
description.p1: An online world clock that displays the current time in 70+ major cities worldwide in real-time. With an elegant digital segment design, check time at a glance and compare time zones across cities. Perfect for international meetings, overseas trading, travel planning, ticketing, and any situation requiring precise timing.

worldTime.title: World Time at a Glance

Useful for business trips, international meetings, and calculating time differences for overseas transactions. Drag and drop to arrange your frequently checked cities in your preferred order.

tips.title: Usage Tips
tips.p1: Use for precise timing during concert ticketing, or simulate exam conditions when preparing for tests. Also useful for checking stock exchange opening/closing times for international trading. Your settings are automatically saved in your browser, so you can continue with the same setup on your next visit.

worldTime.q: Which cities can I check the time for?
worldTime.a: You can check the current time in 70+ major cities worldwide including Seoul, Tokyo, New York, London, Paris, and Sydney. Search and add any city you need.

cityCount.q: How many world cities can I add?
cityCount.a: We support over 70 major cities, and you can add as many as you want and reorder them with drag and drop. Place your frequently checked cities at the top.

settings.a: Yes, all settings including theme, added cities, and city order are automatically saved in your browser. You can use the same setup on your next visit.

mobile.q: Can I use it on mobile?
mobile.a: Yes, with responsive design, it works optimally on all devices including PCs, tablets, and smartphones. Fullscreen mode is also supported.

accuracy.q: Is the time accurate? Could it differ from my computer clock?
accuracy.a: This clock uses your device's system time. Most devices automatically sync with internet time servers, providing high accuracy. If you need even more precise server time, please use our dedicated 'Server Time' tool.

ticketing.q: Can I use this clock for ticketing?
ticketing.a: Yes, this online clock displays time accurately to the second. You can start booking at the exact time for concerts, musicals, sports events, and more.

offline.q: Does the clock work without internet?
offline.a: After the initial load, time is calculated within your browser, so the clock works even without internet. However, some features like adding world cities require an internet connection.

privacy.title: Privacy Notice
privacy.text: World clock data is processed in your browser and is not transmitted to any server. All user settings including added cities and theme preferences are stored only in the browser's local storage.

meta.title: Lap Timer with Excel Export - Free Browser Stopwatch
meta.description: Record lap times with millisecond precision and export to Excel. Fullscreen focus mode, keyboard shortcuts, beep sounds. Free stopwatch in your browser.
meta.keywords: lap timer with excel export, stopwatch with lap recording, millisecond stopwatch browser, fullscreen stopwatch for study, stopwatch with sound alert, free lap timer no app
meta.ogTitle: Lap Timer with Excel Export - Free Browser Stopwatch
meta.ogDescription: Record lap times with millisecond precision and export to Excel. Fullscreen mode, keyboard shortcuts, beep sounds. No install needed.

seo.title: Online Stopwatch - Study & Workout Time Measurement
seo.desc: This stopwatch measures up to milliseconds accuracy and can be used for study time tracking, workout lap time measurement, and work time management. Supports fullscreen mode for better focus. Works perfectly on both PC and mobile.
seo.shortDesc: A free online stopwatch with millisecond precision timing.

description.title: What is a Stopwatch?
description.p1: A stopwatch is a time measurement tool that precisely measures elapsed time. It can measure time down to milliseconds (1/100th of a second) from the moment you press start until you stop. Unlike a timer that counts down from a set time to zero, a stopwatch starts from zero and measures time indefinitely.

list.resume: Resume: Continue from where you paused
list.lap: Lap: Record current time while continuing

step1.title: Getting Started
step1.desc: Click the green 'Start' button to begin. Time is displayed in minutes:seconds.milliseconds format.

step2.title: Recording Laps
step2.desc: Press 'Lap' while running to record split times. Useful for comparing different segments.

step3.title: Pause & Resume
step3.desc: Use 'Pause' to stop and 'Resume' to continue measuring from where you left off.

step4.title: Managing Records
step4.desc: Lap records are auto-saved. Export to Excel or delete individual entries.

study.title: Study & Focus
study.desc: Track study time with Pomodoro technique (25 min work + 5 min break) or measure total daily study hours.

fitness.title: Sports & Fitness
fitness.desc: Record workout times and lap splits for running, swimming, cycling. Perfect for interval training.

gaming.title: Game Speedruns
gaming.desc: Measure game completion times and compare segment records. Challenge your personal best.

cooking.title: Cooking Timing
cooking.desc: Perfect for precise cooking times - boiling pasta, grilling steaks, and more.

work.title: Work Productivity
work.desc: Measure time spent on tasks to analyze and improve work efficiency.

exam.title: Test & Competition Prep
exam.desc: Practice solving problems within time limits or rehearse presentations.

diff.q: What's the difference between a stopwatch and a timer?
diff.a: A stopwatch starts from zero and measures elapsed time indefinitely, while a timer counts down from a set time to zero. Use a stopwatch for 'how long did it take' and a timer for 'how much time is left'.

save.q: Will my records be saved if I close the browser?
save.a: Lap records are automatically saved in your browser's local storage. You can access previous records when returning with the same browser. However, clearing browser data will also delete records.

mobile.q: Can I use it on mobile devices?
mobile.a: Yes, with responsive design it works optimally on smartphones, tablets, and PCs.

maxTime.a: There's no time limit - measure indefinitely. Times over 1 hour are displayed in hours:minutes:seconds.milliseconds format.

export.a: Yes, click 'Export Excel' to download all lap records as a CSV file. Open it in Excel or Google Sheets.

privacy.title: Privacy Notice
privacy.text: Stopwatch records are processed only in your browser and are not transmitted to any server. Lap records and settings are stored only in the browser's local storage, so there is no risk of your personal data being leaked.

meta.title: Timer with Alarm Sound - Free Countdown in Browser
meta.description: Set a countdown timer with 5 alarm sounds and vibration alerts. Screen stays on while running. Quick presets for cooking and workouts. No app install needed.
meta.keywords: timer with alarm sound free, countdown timer that keeps screen on, browser timer with vibration alert, quick preset timer for cooking, free timer with loud alarm, countdown timer no app needed
meta.ogTitle: Timer with Alarm Sound - Free Countdown in Browser
meta.ogDescription: Countdown timer with 5 alarm sounds, vibration alerts, and screen wake lock. Quick presets for cooking and workouts. Free, no install.

seo.title: Online Timer & Pomodoro - Workout & Cooking Alarm
seo.desc: Use the Pomodoro technique with 25-min focus / 5-min break cycles, or set Tabata workout and kitchen timers. Features 5 alarm sounds (Classic/Digital/Chime/Bird/School Bell) and vibration alerts.

description.title: What is an Online Timer?
description.p1: An online timer is a free countdown timer you can use directly in your web browser. Set hours, minutes, and seconds freely, and get an alarm when the countdown ends. Use it as a cooking timer, study timer, workout timer, and more.
description.p2: It offers 4 modes (Timer, Pomodoro, Interval, Multi Timer), 5 alarm sounds, 6 ambient sounds, vibration alerts, and voice countdown. No installation required - use it on PC and mobile instantly.

multiMode.title: 4 Modes
multiMode.desc: Switch between Timer, Pomodoro, Interval, and Multi Timer modes using the tabs at the top.

alarmSounds.title: 5 Alarm Sounds & Vibration
alarmSounds.desc: Choose from Classic Bell, Digital Beep, Chime, Bird Song, or School Bell. Vibration and voice countdown also supported.

ambientSounds.title: 6 Ambient Sounds
ambientSounds.desc: Rain, Cafe, White Noise, Fireplace, Ocean Waves, and Forest sounds to help you focus.

presets.title: Presets & URL Sharing
presets.desc: Save frequently used times as presets and share timer settings via URL.

wakeLock.title: Screen Wake Lock
wakeLock.desc: Prevents the screen from turning off while the timer is running. Keyboard shortcuts also supported.

steps.step1: Step 1: Choose Mode — Select Timer, Pomodoro, Interval, or Multi from the tabs at the top.
steps.step2: Step 2: Set Time — Enter hours/minutes/seconds or use preset buttons for quick setup.
steps.step3: Step 3: Alarm & Options — Configure alarm sound, ambient sound, volume, and vibration.
steps.step4: Step 4: Start — Click Start or press Space bar to begin countdown. Extend with +1min/+5min buttons.

cooking.title: Cooking & Kitchen
cooking.desc: Set precise cooking times for noodles (3 min), pasta (8 min), oven recipes, and more.

study.title: Study & Learning
study.desc: Set focus time for efficient studying. Even more effective when combined with Pomodoro mode.

exercise.title: Exercise & Training
exercise.desc: Use Interval mode for Tabata, HIIT, and various workout programs.

work.title: Work & Meetings
work.desc: Manage presentation time, meeting duration, and boost work efficiency.

noSound.q: I can't hear the timer alarm
noSound.a: Please check your browser's volume settings. Some browsers may restrict audio playback before user interaction (like clicking). The alarm will work normally after you click the Start button.

noodles.q: How many minutes should I set for cooking noodles?
noodles.a: Standard instant noodles typically require 3 minutes. Some products may need 4-5 minutes. Enter the desired time in the Minutes field and press Start.

pomodoro.a: Developed by Francesco Cirillo in the 1980s. One 'pomodoro' is 25 minutes of focus + 5 minutes of break. After 4 pomodoros, take a 15-30 minute long break.

pomodoroCustom.q: Can I change the Pomodoro focus time?
pomodoroCustom.a: Yes, use the ± buttons to adjust from 1 to 90 minutes in 5-minute increments. Break times are adjustable too.

tabata.a: Developed by Dr. Izumi Tabata in Japan. It's a HIIT protocol of 20 seconds all-out exercise + 10 seconds rest for 8 sets (4 minutes total).

hiitDiff.q: What's the difference between HIIT and Tabata?
hiitDiff.a: Tabata is a specific type of HIIT. HIIT is the general term for high-intensity interval training with flexible work/rest times and rounds. Tabata is a fixed protocol of 20s/10s/8 rounds.

multiMax.q: How many timers can I run simultaneously?
multiMax.a: You can run up to 4 independent timers simultaneously. Each timer can be started, stopped, and reset individually.

privacy.title: Privacy Notice
privacy.text: Timer settings and records are processed only in your browser and are not transmitted to any server. Pomodoro session records and alarm settings are stored only in the browser's local storage, so there is no risk of your personal data being leaked.

description.title: Countdown Timer
description.p1: A countdown timer that alerts you when time is up. Use quick preset buttons (1m, 3m, 5m, etc.) for one-click start, or save your own custom presets for frequently used times. Extend time on the fly with +1m/+5m buttons for flexible time management.

f1.title: ⏱️ Quick Presets
f1.desc: One-click start with 1m, 3m, 5m, 10m, 15m, 30m, and 1h preset buttons. No manual input needed.

f2.title: 💾 Save Custom Presets
f2.desc: Name and save your frequently used times (up to 10). Load them instantly with a single click next time.

f3.title: 🔗 URL Sharing
f3.desc: Copy your timer settings as a URL to share with others. Recipients can open the link and the same time is instantly set.

f4.title: ⏳ +1m / +5m Extend
f4.desc: Need more time? Extend the running timer instantly with +1 minute or +5 minute buttons.

steps.step1: Step 1 - Set Time: Click the hour/min/sec fields and enter your desired time, or tap a preset button like 1m, 3m, or 5m.
steps.step2: Step 2 - Start: Press the ▶ Start button to begin the countdown. You can also press the Space bar on your keyboard.
steps.step3: Step 3 - Pause & Extend: Pause with the Stop button, or tap +1m/+5m to add more time while running.
steps.step4: Step 4 - Alarm: When time is up, your chosen alarm sound plays and a popup appears. Tap OK to set a new timer.

u1.title: 🍜 Cooking Noodles
u1.desc: Set 3 minutes for ramen, 4 minutes for cup noodles, or 2 minutes for instant rice.

u2.title: 🍳 Cooking & Baking
u2.desc: Track oven preheating, dough rising, pasta boiling, and other cooking steps.

u3.title: 📚 Study & Focus
u3.desc: Set a goal like '30 minutes of study' to boost your concentration.

u4.title: ☕ Brewing Tea & Coffee
u4.desc: Green tea 2m, black tea 3m, pour-over coffee 4m — time your beverages precisely.

q1.q: How do I save a custom preset?
q1.a: Enter your desired time in the hour/min/sec fields, type a name in the input below, and click 'Save'. Your saved preset will appear in the preset list and can be loaded with one click.

q2.a: The chain timer lets you add multiple steps with custom names and durations. Press Start once and they run in sequence automatically — when one step ends, the next begins. Great for complex routines.

q3.q: Can I extend time while the timer is running?
q3.a: Yes! During countdown, +1 minute and +5 minute buttons appear. Click them to instantly add time to the remaining countdown.

q4.q: I can't hear the alarm sound
q4.a: Check your browser's volume settings. Some browsers restrict audio before user interaction. The alarm will work normally after you click the Start button.

description.title: Pomodoro Timer
description.p1: The Pomodoro Technique is a time management method based on '25 minutes of focus → 5 minutes of break' cycles. After 4 sessions, take a 15-minute long break. This timer offers everything you need: adjustable focus/break durations, automatic phase transitions, daily & weekly statistics, and task list management.

f1.title: 🔴 Auto Phase Switching
f1.desc: Automatically transitions from focus to break. Every 4 sessions, a long break (15 min) is applied.

f2.title: ⏰ Adjustable Durations
f2.desc: Customize focus (1-90 min), short break (1-90 min), and long break (1-90 min) in ±5 minute increments.

f3.title: 📊 Session Statistics
f3.desc: Track today's focus sessions, weekly graph, total focus time, and consecutive day streaks at a glance.

f4.title: 📝 Task Management
f4.desc: Add tasks, set estimated pomodoros for each, and track progress with 🍅 badges.

steps.step1: Step 1 - Set Duration: Default is 25 min focus / 5 min break. Use the ± buttons to adjust to your preferred duration.
steps.step2: Step 2 - Add Tasks (Optional): Add your to-do items in the task list below and select the current task. You can set estimated pomodoros too.
steps.step3: Step 3 - Start Focus: Press ▶ Start to begin 🔴 Focus mode. When done, it automatically switches to 🟢 Break.
steps.step4: Step 4 - Check Stats: Completed sessions are automatically recorded. Check your daily and weekly focus stats in the graph.

u1.title: 📖 Exam Study
u1.desc: Maintain focus during long study sessions by alternating 25 min focus with 5 min breaks.

u2.title: 💻 Coding & Development
u2.desc: Set a goal of implementing one feature per pomodoro for systematic development.

u3.title: ✍️ Writing & Reports
u3.desc: Focus 25 minutes on drafting, then refine after a break for efficient writing.

u4.title: 🎯 Remote Work
u4.desc: Register today's tasks in the task list and manage your time in pomodoro units.

q1.a: Developed by Francesco Cirillo in the 1980s. One 'pomodoro' is 25 min focus + 5 min break. After 4 pomodoros, take a 15-30 min long break. Short focus periods with regular breaks maintain high productivity without fatigue.

q2.q: Can I change the focus time from 25 minutes?
q2.a: Yes, use the ± buttons next to the focus time to adjust from 1 to 90 minutes in 5-minute increments. Break times are adjustable too.

q3.a: All statistics are stored in your browser's local storage. Records are kept for the last 90 days and can be viewed anytime in the same browser.

q4.a: When the 'Auto Start' checkbox is enabled, break starts automatically after focus ends, and the next focus starts automatically after break ends. No need to press buttons — your flow stays uninterrupted.

description.title: Interval Timer
description.p1: An interval timer that automatically alternates between Work and Rest phases for a set number of rounds. Apply Tabata (20s work + 10s rest × 8 rounds) or HIIT (40s work + 20s rest × 6 rounds) presets with one click, or create custom settings with your own times and rounds.

f1.title: 🏃 Tabata Preset
f1.desc: 20s work + 10s rest × 8 rounds. One click applies the globally proven Tabata protocol.

f2.title: 💪 HIIT Preset
f2.desc: 40s work + 20s rest × 6 rounds. Optimized settings for high-intensity interval training.

f3.title: 🔧 Custom Settings
f3.desc: Freely adjust work time (5-300s), rest time (5-120s), and rounds (1-20) to your needs.

f4.title: 🔄 Auto Transition
f4.desc: Work → Rest → Next round transitions happen automatically. Just focus on your exercise!

steps.step1: Step 1 - Choose Preset: Select Tabata, HIIT, or Custom. Custom mode lets you set your own times.
steps.step2: Step 2 - Adjust (Optional): Use ± buttons to fine-tune work time, rest time, and number of rounds.
steps.step3: Step 3 - Start: Press ▶ Start and the 💪 Work phase begins. It automatically switches to 😮‍💨 Rest when done.
steps.step4: Step 4 - Complete Rounds: The alarm sounds when all rounds are done. You can pause or reset at any time.

u1.title: 🏋️ Tabata Workout
u1.desc: Do burpees, squats, mountain climbers — 20s all-out + 10s rest for 8 rounds.

u2.title: 🏃 HIIT Training
u2.desc: Alternate high/low intensity for running, jump rope, cycling, and more.

u3.title: 🧘 Stretching & Yoga
u3.desc: Set custom 30s hold + 10s transition for stretching routines.

u4.title: 🏠 Home Workout
u4.desc: Bodyweight exercises with interval timing for an effective home training session.

q1.a: Developed by Dr. Izumi Tabata in Japan. It's a high-intensity interval training of 20s all-out exercise + 10s rest for 8 sets (4 minutes total). It improves both aerobic and anaerobic capacity in a short time.

q2.q: What's the difference between HIIT and Tabata?
q2.a: Tabata is a specific type of HIIT. HIIT is the general term for high-intensity interval training with flexible work/rest times and rounds. Tabata is a fixed protocol of 20s/10s/8 rounds.

q3.q: Can I change the number of rounds?
q3.a: Yes, use the ± buttons to set anywhere from 1 to 20 rounds. Adjust based on your fitness level.

q4.q: Can I pause during a workout?
q4.a: Yes, press the Stop button or Space bar to pause. Resume to continue from where you left off.

description.title: Multi Timer
description.p1: Run up to 4 independent timers simultaneously. Name each timer and start, stop, or reset them individually. Perfect for managing multiple tasks that need timing at the same time.

f1.title: 🔢 Up to 4 Simultaneous Timers
f1.desc: Add up to 4 independent timers and start/stop each one separately.

f2.title: 🏷️ Custom Names
f2.desc: Label each timer so you can tell at a glance which timer is for what.

f3.title: 🎨 Status Colors
f3.desc: Idle (purple), running (green), completed (red) — each timer's status is color-coded.

f4.title: 🎯 Individual Controls
f4.desc: Each timer has its own start, stop, and reset buttons — completely independent of each other.

steps.step1: Step 1 - Add Timer: Click '+ Add Timer' to create a new timer. You can add up to 4.
steps.step2: Step 2 - Name & Set Time: Enter a name for each timer and set the desired time in the min:sec fields.
steps.step3: Step 3 - Start Individually: Press ▶ on each timer to start them individually. Start only the ones you need first.
steps.step4: Step 4 - Manage: Completed timers turn red with an alarm. Reset to reuse or tap ✕ to remove.

u1.title: 🍳 Complex Cooking
u1.desc: Time pasta 8 min + sauce 15 min + garnish prep 3 min — all cooking steps simultaneously.

u2.title: 🧪 Lab Experiments
u2.desc: When multiple experiment stages run at once, manage each stage with its own timer.

u3.title: 📋 Task Time Management
u3.desc: Track deadlines for 'Check email 10m', 'Write report 30m', 'Meeting prep 15m' all at once.

u4.title: 🎲 Board Games & Quizzes
u4.desc: Countdown time limits for multiple teams or participants simultaneously.

q1.q: How many timers can I add?
q1.a: You can add up to 4 timers. A message will appear if you try to add more than 4.

q2.q: What happens when multiple timers finish at the same time?
q2.a: Each timer triggers its own alarm when complete and turns red. If they finish simultaneously, alarms sound in succession.

q3.q: Can I pause just one timer?
q3.a: Yes, each timer has its own stop button. Pause any timer while the others keep running.

q4.q: Can I switch tabs while using multi timer?
q4.a: You cannot switch to other tabs (Timer/Pomodoro/Interval) while multi timers are running. Stop or remove all timers first to switch.

meta.title: Pomodoro Timer with Ambient Sounds & Stats Tracker
meta.description: Focus with 25/5 Pomodoro cycles plus ambient background sounds. Auto session switching, productivity stats, and task tracking. Free, works in browser.
meta.keywords: pomodoro timer with ambient sounds, pomodoro with statistics tracker, auto switching pomodoro timer, pomodoro with task list free, focus timer with background sounds, pomodoro timer no sign up
meta.ogTitle: Pomodoro Timer with Ambient Sounds & Stats
meta.ogDescription: 25/5 Pomodoro cycles with ambient sounds, auto session switching, and productivity statistics. Free browser tool, no sign up.

description.title: What is the Pomodoro Technique?
description.p1: The Pomodoro Technique is a time management methodology developed by Francesco Cirillo in Italy in the 1980s. It involves focusing on a single task for 25 minutes, followed by a 5-minute break, and taking a 15-30 minute long break after 4 cycles.
description.p2: This online Pomodoro timer provides features optimized for the Pomodoro Technique, including automatic session switching, statistics tracking, and ambient sound support. Use it directly in your browser with no installation required.

autoSwitch.title: Auto Session Switching
autoSwitch.desc: Automatically transitions between focus → break → focus. A long break starts after 4 sessions.

customTime.title: Flexible Time Settings
customTime.desc: Adjust focus time (1-90 min) and break time in 5-minute increments to find your optimal duration.

statistics.title: Pomodoro Statistics
statistics.desc: Automatically tracks completed pomodoros and total focus time to objectively measure your productivity.

ambientSound.title: 6 Ambient Sounds
ambientSound.desc: Rain, Cafe, White Noise, Fireplace, Ocean Waves, and Forest sounds to help you concentrate.

taskList.title: Task List
taskList.desc: Manage your to-do list and clearly set which task to work on during each Pomodoro session.

steps.step1: Step 1: Set Time — Use the default 25 min focus / 5 min break or adjust with ± buttons.
steps.step2: Step 2: Configure — Choose alarm and ambient sounds, add tasks to your to-do list.
steps.step3: Step 3: Start Focus — Press Start and immerse yourself in a single task.
steps.step4: Step 4: Break & Repeat — When the alarm rings, take a 5 min break. Take a long break after 4 sessions.

study.title: Study & Exam Prep
study.desc: Maximize study efficiency with 25-minute focus sessions for exams and certifications.

coding.title: Coding & Development
coding.desc: Maintain focus during programming and creative work while preventing burnout.

reading.title: Reading & Writing
reading.desc: Use for reading, report writing, blog posts, and other tasks requiring sustained concentration.

exam.title: Practice Tests
exam.desc: Simulate actual test conditions with time limits and manage break times between sections.

whatIs.a: Developed by Francesco Cirillo in the 1980s. One 'pomodoro' is 25 minutes of focus + 5 minutes of break. After 4 pomodoros, take a 15-30 minute long break. Short focus periods with regular breaks maintain high productivity without fatigue.

changeTime.q: Can I change the focus time from 25 minutes?
changeTime.a: Yes, use the ± buttons to adjust from 1 to 90 minutes in 5-minute increments. Break times are adjustable too. Find the optimal duration that works for you.

statistics.q: How do I check my Pomodoro statistics?
statistics.a: Statistics are automatically recorded each time you complete a session. Check completed pomodoros and total focus time to objectively track your productivity.

tabClose.q: Does the timer stop if I close the tab?
tabClose.a: Yes, completely closing the browser tab stops the timer. However, using other tabs or locking the screen while keeping the tab open will not affect the timer.

ambientSound.a: Yes, we offer 6 ambient sounds: Rain, Cafe, White Noise, Fireplace, Ocean Waves, and Forest. Background sounds help block external noise and boost focus.

free.a: Yes, the Pomodoro Timer is completely free with no registration or installation required. Use it directly in your browser.

privacy.title: Privacy Notice
privacy.text: All Pomodoro timer settings and statistics are stored only in your browser's localStorage. No data is transmitted to any server. Use it with confidence.

meta.title: HIIT Interval Timer with Custom Rounds - Free Online
meta.description: Set custom work/rest intervals and rounds for HIIT, Tabata, and circuit training. Built-in presets, voice countdown, vibration alerts. Free, no app needed.
meta.keywords: HIIT interval timer custom rounds, tabata timer with voice countdown, interval timer with vibration alert, circuit training timer free, custom work rest timer online, interval timer with presets free
meta.ogTitle: HIIT Interval Timer with Custom Rounds
meta.ogDescription: Custom work/rest intervals and rounds for HIIT and Tabata. Voice countdown, vibration alerts, built-in presets. Free in your browser.

description.title: What is an Interval Timer?
description.p1: An interval timer is a timer optimized for interval training that alternates between exercise and rest periods. It supports various workout protocols including Tabata (20s work/10s rest/8 rounds) and HIIT (High-Intensity Interval Training).
description.p2: Freely set work time, rest time, and number of rounds, or use built-in Tabata and HIIT presets. No installation needed - use it directly in your browser.

tabataPreset.title: Tabata Preset
tabataPreset.desc: One-click setup for the authentic Tabata protocol: 20s work / 10s rest / 8 rounds.

customSettings.title: Custom Settings
customSettings.desc: Freely set work time (5s-10min), rest time (5s-5min), and rounds (1-50) for your workout.

autoSwitch.title: Auto Switching
autoSwitch.desc: Automatically cycles work → rest → work with current round and remaining time displayed prominently.

alarmSound.title: 5 Alarm Sounds
alarmSound.desc: Choose from 5 alarm sounds for work/rest transitions. Vibration alerts also supported.

wakeLock.title: Screen Wake Lock
wakeLock.desc: Prevents the screen from turning off during workouts. Use with confidence on smartphones.

steps.step1: Step 1: Preset or Custom — Select Tabata (20s/10s/8 rounds) or HIIT preset, or set custom times and rounds.
steps.step2: Step 2: Configure Alarm — Choose alarm sound for work/rest transitions, set volume and vibration.
steps.step3: Step 3: Start Workout — Press Start for automatic work → rest → work cycling.
steps.step4: Step 4: Complete — Completion alarm sounds when all rounds are done. Check your total workout time.

tabata.title: Tabata Workouts
tabata.desc: Optimized for authentic Tabata: 20 seconds all-out exercise + 10 seconds rest for 8 sets.

hiit.title: HIIT Training
hiit.desc: Set your own high-intensity interval training program with custom work/rest intervals.

crossfit.title: CrossFit & Circuits
crossfit.desc: Use for CrossFit WODs, circuit training, and various workout programs.

running.title: Running & Cardio
running.desc: Interval running (sprints + jogging) to effectively improve endurance and fitness.

whatIsTabata.a: Developed by Dr. Izumi Tabata in Japan, it's a HIIT protocol of 20 seconds all-out exercise + 10 seconds rest for 8 sets (4 minutes total). It improves both aerobic and anaerobic capacity in a short time.

hiitDiff.q: What's the difference between HIIT and Tabata?
hiitDiff.a: Tabata is a specific type of HIIT. HIIT is the general term for high-intensity interval training with flexible work/rest times and rounds. Tabata is a fixed protocol of 20s work / 10s rest / 8 rounds.

customSettings.q: Can I customize work/rest times and rounds?
customSettings.a: Yes, freely set work time (5s-10min), rest time (5s-5min), and rounds (1-50). Tabata and basic HIIT presets are also available.

pause.q: Can I pause during an interval?
pause.a: Yes, press the pause button at any time during work or rest. Resume to continue from where you stopped.

mobile.q: Does it work on mobile devices?
mobile.a: Yes, it works perfectly on smartphones and tablets with responsive design that adapts to any screen size.

free.a: Yes, the Interval Timer is completely free with no registration or installation required.

privacy.title: Privacy Notice
privacy.text: All interval timer settings are stored only in your browser's localStorage. No data is transmitted to any server. Use it with confidence.

description.p2: Perfect for managing cooking times for multiple dishes, setting workout timers, or tracking deadlines for various tasks. No installation needed - use it directly in your browser.

multiRun.title: Simultaneous Execution
multiRun.desc: Run up to 4 timers simultaneously. Each timer operates completely independently.

individual.title: Individual Control

alarmSound.title: 5 Alarm Sounds

description.title: What is an Online Alarm Clock?
description.p1: An online alarm clock is a free alarm service that works directly in your web browser without any app installation. You can easily set and manage alarms on any device with an internet connection — PC, laptop, tablet, or smartphone.
description.p2: It offers practical features including 5 different alarm sounds, multiple alarm support, snooze functionality, and quick presets. All settings are automatically saved in your browser and persist between visits.

multiAlarm.title: Multiple Alarms
multiAlarm.desc: Set and manage up to 10 alarms simultaneously. Label each alarm to distinguish its purpose.

sounds.title: 15 Alarm Sounds
sounds.desc: Choose from 15 diverse alarm sounds including Soft Bells, Arpeggio, Swinging Jazz, Rooster, and more.

presets.title: Quick Presets
presets.desc: One-click alarm setup with buttons for 10 minutes, 30 minutes, 1 hour, or 2 hours from now.

snooze.title: Snooze Function
snooze.desc: When the alarm rings, press the snooze button to be reminded again in 5 minutes.

notification.title: Browser Notifications
notification.desc: Receive alarm notifications even when the tab is inactive through browser notifications.

steps.step1: Step 1: Set Time — Enter the hour and minute for the alarm, or use the quick preset buttons.
steps.step2: Step 2: Choose Sound — Select one of the 5 alarm sounds and preview it to confirm.
steps.step3: Step 3: Add Alarm — Click the 'Add Alarm' button to activate it. Optionally add a label.
steps.step4: Step 4: Manage Alarm — When the alarm rings, choose 'Dismiss' or 'Snooze (5 min)' to respond.

nap.title: Power Nap Alarm
nap.desc: Use quick presets to set a 10-30 minute alarm for a refreshing short nap.

medicine.title: Medication Reminder
medicine.desc: Set precise times for medication and use labels to distinguish different prescriptions.

cooking.title: Cooking Timer
cooking.desc: Set alarms matching cooking times so you never miss the perfect moment.

meeting.title: Meeting Reminder
meeting.desc: Set alarms before important meetings or events to ensure you have preparation time.

tabClose.q: Will the alarm work if I close the tab?
tabClose.a: No, closing the browser tab completely will stop the alarm. However, the alarm will ring even if you're using other tabs. Enable browser notifications to receive alerts even when the tab is inactive.

noSound.q: What if the alarm sound doesn't play?
noSound.a: Make sure your browser sound is not muted. Also check that your device volume is high enough and no Bluetooth headphones are connected unexpectedly. On first use, you may need to click the page once to enable sound.

multiple.a: Yes, you can set up to 10 alarms simultaneously. Each alarm can have different times, sounds, and labels for various purposes.

mobile.q: Does it work on mobile devices?
mobile.a: Yes, it works perfectly on smartphones and tablets with mobile browsers. The responsive design adapts to any screen size. Note that some mobile browsers may limit alarms in power-saving mode.

save.a: Yes, all alarm settings are automatically saved in your browser's localStorage. When you revisit on the same device and browser, your previously set alarms will be preserved.

free.a: Yes, the online alarm clock is completely free with no registration or installation required. Ads may be displayed, but all features are available without any restrictions.

privacy.title: Privacy Notice
privacy.text: This online alarm clock stores your alarm settings only in your browser's localStorage. No data is sent to any server. There is no personal data collection whatsoever, so you can use it with confidence.

meta.title: Exact Server Time in Milliseconds - Sync & Countdown
meta.description: See NTP-synced server time with millisecond precision. Set countdown to an exact moment for ticket drops, product launches, or registration deadlines. Free tool.
meta.keywords: exact server time milliseconds, NTP synced clock online, countdown to exact time for tickets, precise time for ticket drops, millisecond accurate clock browser, server time sync tool free
meta.ogTitle: Exact Server Time in Milliseconds - Sync & Countdown
meta.ogDescription: NTP-synced server time with millisecond precision. Countdown to exact moments for ticket drops and launches. Free browser tool.

description.title: What is Server Time / Ticketing Countdown?
description.p1: Server Time is a free online tool that syncs with worldtimeapi.org to provide precise current time in milliseconds (ms). It compensates for network latency to show more accurate time than your computer clock.
description.p2: The ticketing countdown feature lets you set a target time and watch the countdown in milliseconds — perfect for concert ticket sales, course registration, limited edition purchases, and any situation where precise timing matters. When the target time arrives, a 'GO!' indicator appears with visual feedback.

preciseTime.title: Millisecond Precision
preciseTime.desc: Smooth millisecond (ms) time display using requestAnimationFrame for precise current time verification.

serverSync.title: Server Time Sync
serverSync.desc: Syncs with worldtimeapi.org server with network latency compensation. Check sync status and offset in real-time.

ticketing.title: Ticketing Countdown
ticketing.desc: Set a target time and watch the countdown in milliseconds. 'GO!' indicator with visual feedback when the target is reached.

copyTime.title: Copy Time
copyTime.desc: Copy the current time in HH:MM:SS.mmm format to your clipboard with one click.

darkMode.title: Dark Mode Support
darkMode.desc: Supports both light and dark themes for comfortable use in any environment.

steps.step1: Step 1: Check Server Time — Upon loading, the page automatically syncs with the server and displays precise time in milliseconds.
steps.step2: Step 2: Set Target Time — In ticketing mode, enter target time (HH:MM:SS) or use quick preset buttons.
steps.step3: Step 3: Start Countdown — Press 'Start Countdown' to see remaining time displayed in real-time with millisecond precision.
steps.step4: Step 4: Confirm GO! — When the target time arrives, 'GO!' appears on screen. Act at the precise moment.

ticketing.title: Concert/Event Ticketing
ticketing.desc: Increase your ticketing success rate with precise countdown to popular event ticket releases.

meeting.title: Course Registration
meeting.desc: Check precise server time for university course registration, exam sign-ups, and other first-come-first-served situations.

broadcast.title: Live Streams/Events
broadcast.desc: Use for live broadcast starts, limited edition releases, and other events requiring precise timing.

development.title: Development/Debugging
development.desc: Use precise server time for API response timing, log timestamp verification, and other development tasks.

accuracy.q: How accurate is the server time?
accuracy.a: Synced with worldtimeapi.org server for millisecond precision. Network latency is compensated to minimize offset from actual server time.

tabClose.q: Will the countdown persist if I close the tab?
tabClose.a: No, closing the browser tab resets the countdown. It works normally while the tab is open.

mobile.q: Does it work on mobile devices?
mobile.a: Yes, with responsive design it works perfectly on smartphones and tablets.

free.a: Yes, completely free with no registration or installation required.

sync.a: On page load, a request is sent to worldtimeapi.org's NTP-synced server. Round-trip time is measured to compensate for offset.

privacy.title: Privacy Notice
privacy.text: This server time tool sends a request to worldtimeapi.org for time synchronization, but does not collect or store any personal information. All countdown settings are processed only in browser memory and are not transmitted to any server.

description.title: What is D-day Countdown?
description.p1: D-day Countdown is an online tool that lets you track the days remaining until important dates in real-time. Register events like exams, birthdays, anniversaries, travel dates, and project deadlines to manage your D-days at a glance.
description.p2: Future events are displayed as D-N (days remaining), today's events as D-Day, and past events as D+N (days elapsed). With real-time countdown showing days, hours, minutes, and seconds, you can track exact remaining time for up to 20 simultaneous events.

multiEvent.title: Multi-Event Management
multiEvent.desc: Register and manage up to 20 D-day events simultaneously. Sort by date or name for efficient tracking.

liveCountdown.title: Real-time Countdown
liveCountdown.desc: Beyond just day counts, get real-time countdown in hours, minutes, and seconds for precise time tracking.

categories.title: 6 Categories
categories.desc: Organize events into 6 categories: General, Birthday, Anniversary, Exam, Travel, and Work for systematic management.

persistent.title: Auto Save
persistent.desc: All events are automatically saved in your browser's localStorage, so your data persists even after closing the page.

share.title: Copy & Share
share.desc: Copy D-day information as text to easily share via messenger or social media.

steps.step1: Add Event: Click 'Add Event' and enter the title, target date, and category. You can optionally add a memo.
steps.step2: Choose Category: Select from General, Birthday, Anniversary, Exam, Travel, or Work to categorize your event.
steps.step3: Track in Real-time: Registered events are automatically calculated with real-time countdown. Displayed as D-N (future), D-Day (today), or D+N (past).
steps.step4: Manage & Share: Edit or delete events, and copy as text to share. Use sorting for efficient management.

exam.title: Exam Preparation
exam.desc: Register D-days for college entrance exams, certifications, and professional tests to plan your study schedule with real-time remaining time.

anniversary.title: Anniversary Management
anniversary.desc: Register important dates like relationship anniversaries, wedding anniversaries, and family birthdays so you never miss a special day.

travel.title: Travel Planning
travel.desc: Add excitement with a countdown to your departure date and systematically manage your preparation timeline.

project.title: Project Deadlines
project.desc: Register work project deadlines, presentation dates, and submission dates to effectively manage your timelines.

maxEvents.q: How many D-days can I register?
maxEvents.a: You can register and manage up to 20 D-day events simultaneously.

dataSave.a: Yes, all D-day events are automatically saved in your browser's localStorage. They persist between visits on the same device and browser.

mobile.q: Does it work on mobile devices?
mobile.a: Yes, with responsive design it works perfectly on smartphones and tablets.

free.a: Yes, completely free with no registration or installation required.

past.a: Yes, past dates are displayed as D+N showing how many days have passed. They are not automatically deleted, so you can check anniversaries.

privacy.title: Privacy Notice
privacy.text: This D-day countdown tool stores all data only in your browser's localStorage and does not transmit anything to a server. Event information (title, date, category, memo) is processed only on your device.

meta.title: Privacy Policy | Clock-Tani
meta.description: Privacy Policy for Clock-Tani online clock service. Learn about the information we collect, how we use it, and your rights.

Privacy.title: Privacy Policy
Privacy.intro: Clock-Tani (the 'Service') values your privacy and complies with applicable laws. This Privacy Policy explains what information we collect and how we use it.

collect.title: 1. Information We Collect
collect.content: The Service does not require registration and does not directly collect personal identifying information such as names, emails, or phone numbers.
items: Automatically collected information: Browser type, access time, page views, device information (collected anonymously via Google Analytics)
Locally stored data: Clock settings, themes, language preferences are stored only in your browser's localStorage and are never transmitted to our servers

purpose.title: 2. How We Use Information
purpose.content: Collected information is used only for the following purposes:
items: Analyzing usage statistics to improve the service
Ensuring stable service operation and addressing errors

thirdParty.title: 3. Third-Party Services
thirdParty.content: The Service uses the following third-party services:
items: Google Analytics: Collects visitor statistics (uses cookies). Google's Privacy Policy applies.
Google AdSense: Displays personalized ads (uses cookies). You can disable personalized ads in Google Ad Settings.

cookies.title: 4. Cookies
cookies.content: The Service does not create cookies directly, but Google Analytics and Google AdSense may use cookies. You can block cookies in your browser settings, which may limit some functionality.

retention.title: 5. Data Retention & Deletion
retention.content: Data stored in localStorage is immediately removed when you clear your browser data. Google Analytics data is subject to Google's retention policies.

rights.title: 6. Your Rights
rights.content: You can view and delete localStorage data at any time through your browser's developer tools or settings. You have the right to block cookies and disable ad personalization.

changes.title: 7. Policy Changes
changes.content: Any changes to this policy will be posted on this page.

contact.title: 8. Contact
contact.content: For privacy-related inquiries, please contact us at the email below.

meta.title: Terms of Service | Clock-Tani
meta.description: Terms of Service for Clock-Tani online clock service. Service usage conditions, user rights, disclaimers, and copyright policies explained.

Terms.title: Terms of Service
Terms.intro: These Terms of Service govern your use of Clock-Tani (the 'Service'). By using the Service, you agree to these terms.

service.title: 1. Service Overview
service.content: The Service is a free web application providing time-related tools including online clocks, stopwatch, timer, alarm, server time, and D-Day countdown.

usage.title: 2. Terms of Use
usage.content: The Service is free for everyone. No registration is required.
items: You must not use the Service for illegal purposes
You must not interfere with the normal operation of the Service
You must not reproduce or distribute the Service's content without authorization

disclaimer.title: 3. Disclaimer
disclaimer.content: While we strive for accuracy, we do not guarantee the following:
items: Absolute accuracy of displayed times (variations may occur due to network latency, device performance, etc.)
Uninterrupted service operation (temporary outages may occur for maintenance or technical issues)
Precise timer/alarm notifications (delays may occur when browser tabs are inactive)

liability.title: 4. Limitation of Liability
liability.content: The Service provider is not legally liable for any direct or indirect damages arising from use of the Service. For critical time management (exams, work, etc.), we recommend using multiple clocks.

ip.title: 5. Intellectual Property
ip.content: All design, code, and content of the Service are the intellectual property of the Service provider. Use is permitted for personal, non-commercial purposes only.

changes.title: 6. Changes to Terms
changes.content: These terms may be updated as needed. Changes will be posted on this page.

contact.title: 7. Contact
contact.content: For questions about these terms, please contact us at the email below.
